frame_version: v2 -- doc-density stratified (supersedes v1 star-stratified frame at commit 05db7ca)
frame_status_note: Rebuilt per GGs correction: star-stratification held documentation quality roughly constant across strata, so it did not sample the documented-vs-under-documented axis the papers thesis is about. This frame re-stratifies the SAME 30 structurally-vetted candidates (frame v1, commit 05db7ca) by MECHANICAL doc-density (agentgauge/exp1_doc_density.py), computed from real extracted tool definitions (agentgauge/exp1_tool_def_extractor.py), not README prose or star count. Do NOT begin mirroring/scoring against this frame until GG explicitly ratifies or overrides the open decisions below.
pre_registration_amendment_note: This frame construction deviates from the pre-reg original 5-source, star-stratified sampling plan on two axes -- both amended explicitly in exp1_pre_registration.json's amendments section, not silently: (1) single-source (GitHub topic search only), (2) doc-density stratification, not star-based.
predecessor_frame_note: v1 (star-stratified, superseded) available at git commit 05db7ca for audit trail.
discovery_source: GitHub topic:mcp-server, 500 repos fetched (unchanged from v1 -- same 30 structurally-vetted candidates, re-scored not re-discovered)
vetting_method_note: Each candidate was screened by an independent research pass against two criteria GG specified: (a) is_focused_mcp_server -- primary purpose is exposing a FIXED MCP tool catalog, not a general platform/framework/aggregator/gateway/SDK-for-building-servers with MCP as an incidental feature; (b) approx_tool_count >= 5, so a confusable family (2+ similarly-named/purposed tools) can plausibly exist. ~55 candidates were screened across the 500-repo discovery pool; 30 passed. A previously-unanticipated failure mode was found and excluded throughout: aggregators/gateways/proxies that dynamically route to OTHER MCP servers rather than exposing their own fixed catalog (e.g. metatool-ai/metamcp, tadata-org/fastapi_mcp) -- these have no stable docstring surface to mirror.

doc_density_methodology.scorer: agentgauge/exp1_doc_density.py compute_doc_density() -- 30% description length, 25% description presence, 25% non-echo descriptions, 20% param-description coverage
doc_density_methodology.extractor: agentgauge/exp1_tool_def_extractor.py -- deterministic, no LLM/agent: git clone --depth 1, static parsing (Python AST decorator + imperative add_tool() + JSON-literal fallback; regex proximity for other languages)
doc_density_methodology.tiering: assign_tiers() -- empirical terciles (p33/p67) of composite_score WITHIN the 23 successfully-scored fresh candidates. Tier names (well_documented/thin/near_empty) are RELATIVE to this sample, not absolute doc-quality bands -- see honest_caveat below.

open_decisions_applied_by_default.issue: Mechanical extraction failed (0 tools extracted) for 7 of the 30 structurally-vetted fresh candidates, despite all 7 having >=13 tools per the manual vetting pass (approx_tool_count) -- confirmed extraction-method gaps (2x Java: no annotation-based pattern implemented; 1x confirmed YAML-manifest architecture, not source code; 4x C/Go/TS/unknown-language misses), not real near-empty signal. Full detail + per-server reasons in exp1_exclusion_log.json. This leaves only 23 fresh candidates with a valid doc-density score, short of the pre-registered freshly_sampled_count=28 by 5.
open_decisions_applied_by_default.default_applied: Proceed with N=23 fresh (scored) + 2 anchors = 25 total as the ACTUAL measured frame. Terciles computed over the 23. The 7 exclusions are logged, not silently dropped.
alternatives_not_applied: Backfill 5-7 replacements from the already-fetched backup pool (70/stratum, evals/fixtures/exp1_candidate_list.json backup_pool) -- REQUIRES a fresh manual vetting pass (is_focused_mcp_server + approx_tool_count>=5 judgment) on backup candidates, since the backup pool is raw/unvetted (e.g. contains n8n-io/n8n, a general automation platform, exactly the pattern excluded during v1 vetting). Deferred: this is judgment work, not mechanical, and doing it now risks the same selection-effect problem being corrected if done casually.

Extend the extractor to cover Java annotation-based registration and/or the YAML-manifest pattern -- deferred as scope creep for this pass (per this session's explicit redirect against per-language extractor rabbit-holing); would only recover up to 2 of the 7 (the 2 Java servers; no clean win on the YAML-manifest one, which is repo-specific).

open_decisions_applied_by_default.issue: The composite-score distribution across the 23 scoreable fresh candidates is heavily right-skewed: range 25.0-100.0, 7 of 23 tied at exactly 100.0 (possible ceiling/saturation in the metric), median ~80. Empirical terciles place 8 servers in near_empty by construction, but only 3 of those 8 are near-empty in an ABSOLUTE sense (pct_tools_with_description < 0.20 -- i.e. genuinely lack descriptions on most tools). The other 6 near_empty-tier servers have 86-100% of tools WITH a description (e.g. chrisryugj-korean-law-mcp: 115 tools, 100% described, composite=80.0 -- tiered near_empty purely because it falls in the bottom third of THIS specific sample, not because it is thin).
open_decisions_applied_by_default.default_applied: Keep the pre-committed tercile methodology (assign_tiers() was fixed before this run, per pre-reg discipline -- not tuned post-hoc). Flag the relative-vs-absolute distinction explicitly here and in the papers methods section rather than silently treating near_empty as if it meant absolutely thin.
alternatives_not_applied: Redefine tiers by absolute composite-score thresholds instead of empirical terciles -- would change the pre-committed methodology after seeing results (exactly the never-tune-to-a-positive pre-reg violation the frozen protocol prohibits).
open_decisions_applied_by_default.reportable_finding: This is itself a confirmation, on a MECHANICAL (not README-based) doc-density measure, of the same finding that motivated this frame's rebuild: GitHub-discoverable, structurally-valid MCP servers (>=5 tools, focused catalog) overwhelmingly ship non-empty tool descriptions. Only 2 of 23 scored servers (vitali87-code-graph-rag, lucasastorian-llmwiki) have 0% description coverage -- genuinely near-empty. The population may lack a large truly-thin-doc segment even under this stricter, non-circular measure.

star_stratified_v1_ref: git commit 05db7ca:evals/fixtures/exp1_server_frame.json (superseded, kept for audit trail)
next_step: Awaiting GG ratification of the two open_decisions_applied_by_default entries above. No mirroring/scoring has begun.
